assistant_message:
I need to refresh internal project references before I continue; this does not ask you to approve a changed scope.
Plainly: I am checking that I use the right project context, produce the output in the right format, and stay inside the work you actually want. You do not need to know the workflow terms; answer the questions in normal language.
I will summarize the relevant file contents here. Links and file paths are supporting evidence, not homework for you.
Important: your approval applies only to the item or items shown in this message. If I create a new format, evidence/boundary set, tool choice, work brief, or start confirmation later, I must show what is inside it and ask again unless you already gave a broader scope.
1. Refresh project evidence and boundaries (CAP-PROFILE-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY)
   Why it matters: The evidence behind this internal capability record changed. I must rebuild it before relying on it; refreshing does not broaden permissions.
   What is inside this item:
   - Previous scope: scope project, phase implementation
   - Changed evidence: capability profile CAP-PROFILE-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY source .codex-plugin/plugin.json changed after record creation, capability profile CAP-PROFILE-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY source .sdlc/requirements/REQ-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY-001.json changed after record creation, capability profile CAP-PROFILE-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY source bin/agentic-sdlc.mjs changed after record creation, capability profile CAP-PROFILE-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY source docs/architecture.md changed after record creation, capability profile CAP-PROFILE-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY source package.json changed after record creation, capability profile CAP-PROFILE-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY source test/cli.e2e.mjs changed after record creation
   - Current source files: .codex-plugin/plugin.json, .sdlc/requirements/REQ-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY-001.json, bin/agentic-sdlc.mjs, docs/architecture.md, package.json, test/cli.e2e.mjs
   If you say yes: No new approval is created by this refresh. A material boundary change still requires a decision.
   Scope of your answer: it applies only to Refresh project evidence and boundaries (CAP-PROFILE-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY). It does not approve later format, tool, brief, or start decisions unless I show them or you already gave a broader scope.
   After that: After refresh, the current policy or combined proposal determines whether a fresh approval is needed.

assistant_message_presentation.instruction: Before showing assistant_message to a human, translate and contextualize it in the active chat language. Use plain product language and begin with outcome, practical impact, the decision needed or an explicit statement that none is needed, what remains protected, and one next action. Do not place record IDs, internal autonomy terms, status or reason codes, hashes, schema keys, file paths, or CLI commands in that primary explanation. Preserve those literals exactly only after an optional Technical details or Dettagli tecnici divider. If an internal freshness check needs a refresh but the user-approved scope has not changed, explain that the internal reference will be updated and work will continue inside the same scope; do not present it as a new product decision. Summarize relevant contents directly instead of sending the user to inspect files. State what approval covers and what it does not cover. A yes or ok applies only to the displayed decision unless the user explicitly grants a broader approval level; later delegated approvals must be recorded as automation, not misattributed as direct user actions.

human_guidance.result: The checks completed without a blocking issue.
human_guidance.impact: The recorded limits and proof are consistent enough for the next separately approved step. The check itself did not change, release, deploy, or merge anything.
human_guidance.required_decision: Decide whether to start the next step; a protected step still needs its own approval.
human_guidance.protection_boundary: These checks did not approve or perform a change, merge, release, deployment, production access, secret access, or work outside the approved files.
human_guidance.next_action: Continue only if the next action is within the approved limits and has any separate approval it requires.
